name: Dual Dagger
description: Dual stereo filter with independent LPF and HPF cutoff controls. Four analog -24dB/oct filters, assignable resonance, stereo pan CV per filter type, and Link mode for 8th-order stereo bandpass.

controls.name: LPF Potentiometer
controls.description: Sets cutoff frequency of both low-pass filters (channels 1 and 2).

controls.name: Link Switch
controls.description: Links LPF to follow HPF, creating an 8th-order stereo bandpass. HPF = bandpass frequency, LPF = bandwidth.

controls.name: Resonance Potentiometer
controls.description: Shared resonance control. Applies to HPF, LPF, or both per switch settings. Jumper on back sets Lo (no self-oscillation) or Hi (self-oscillation allowed) range.

controls.name: HP Resonance Switch
controls.description: Engages resonance on the high-pass filters.
controls.detail: ON=resonance applied to HPF1 and HPF2

controls.name: LP Resonance Switch
controls.description: Engages resonance on the low-pass filters.
controls.detail: ON=resonance applied to LPF1 and LPF2

controls.name: HPF Potentiometer
controls.description: Sets cutoff frequency of both high-pass filters (channels 1 and 2). In Link mode, controls center frequency of bandpass.

inputs.name: IN2
inputs.description: Audio input for channel 2. IN1 normalled to IN2.

inputs.name: LPF CV
inputs.description: CV input for LPF cutoff frequency.

inputs.name: HPF CV
inputs.description: CV input for HPF cutoff frequency.

inputs.name: PANLP CV
inputs.description: Pan CV for LPF: positive voltage opens LPF1 and closes LPF2 (opposite for negative).

inputs.name: PANHP CV
inputs.description: Pan CV for HPF: differentiates HPF cutoff between channels 1 and 2.

outputs.name: OUT1
outputs.description: Filtered audio output for channel 1 (HPF then LPF in series).

outputs.name: OUT2
outputs.description: Filtered audio output for channel 2 (HPF then LPF in series).

behaviors.name: Dual Stereo Filter
behaviors.description: Each audio channel passes through HPF then LPF in series. All four filters are -24dB/oct analog. LPF and HPF frequencies are identical across both channels for precise stereo tracking.

behaviors.name: Bandpass via Link
behaviors.description: LPF follows HPF. HPF control sets bandpass center frequency, LPF control sets bandwidth. Creates an 8th-order stereo bandpass with resonance assignable to HP and/or LP edges.

behaviors.name: Stereo Pan / Detuning
behaviors.description: Pan CV inputs offset the cutoff frequency of channel 1 vs channel 2 in opposite directions. Positive voltage to PANLP opens LPF1 and closes LPF2, creating stereo filter divergence.

behaviors.name: Self-Oscillation VCO
behaviors.trigger: Hi jumper + Resonance max + HPF CV
behaviors.description: With Hi jumper set and resonance at maximum, the HPF section self-oscillates as a sine-wave VCO tracking V/Oct over several octaves.

patchIdeas.name: Stereo bandpass sweep
patchIdeas.patch: Link ON · LFO → HPF CV · LPF set bandwidth · resonance on both edges
patchIdeas.diagram:
DUAL DAGGER:
* Link: ON
* HP Resonance: ON
* LP Resonance: ON
- VCO L (OUT) -> Dual Dagger (IN1)
- VCO R (OUT) -> Dual Dagger (IN2)
- LFO (CV) >> Dual Dagger (HPF CV) // sweeps bandpass center
- Dual Dagger (OUT1) -> Mixer (L)
- Dual Dagger (OUT2) -> Mixer (R)

patchIdeas.name: Dual resonant peak
patchIdeas.patch: Mono → IN1 · mix OUT1+OUT2 · PANLP CV sets distance between two resonant LP peaks
patchIdeas.diagram:
DUAL DAGGER:
* LP Resonance: ON
- Noise (OUT) -> Dual Dagger (IN1) // IN2 normalled from IN1
- LFO (CV) >> Dual Dagger (PANLP CV) // splits the two LP peaks
- Dual Dagger (OUT1) -> Mixer (CH1)
- Dual Dagger (OUT2) -> Mixer (CH2)

patchIdeas.name: Sine VCO voice
patchIdeas.patch: Hi jumper · resonance max → HPF self-oscillates · LPF smooths · second channel FM via PANHP
patchIdeas.diagram:
DUAL DAGGER:
* HP Resonance: ON (Hi jumper, max)
- Keyboard (Pitch) p> Dual Dagger (HPF CV) // V/Oct tracking
- LFO (CV) >> Dual Dagger (PANHP CV) // FM channel 2
- Dual Dagger (OUT1) -> Output (L)
- Dual Dagger (OUT2) -> Output (R)
